name: azure-cosmos-db-py
description: Build Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL services with Python/FastAPI following production-grade patterns. Use when implementing database client setup with dual auth (DefaultAzureCredential + emulator), service layer classes with CRUD operations, partition key strategies, parameterized queries, or TDD patterns for Cosmos. Triggers on phrases like "Cosmos DB", "NoSQL database", "document store", "add persistence", "database service layer", or "Python Cosmos SDK".
capabilities: Set up Cosmos DB client with dual authentication (DefaultAzureCredential for production, emulator key for local development)
Implement five-tier Pydantic model hierarchy for clean separation of concerns
Create service layer with graceful degradation when Cosmos DB is unavailable
Write parameterized queries to prevent injection attacks
Mock Cosmos DB containers for unit testing without actual database connection
Handle partition key validation and cross-partition queries
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 37 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanned file (SKILL.md) is a documentation file containing code examples in markdown format, not executable code. External command detections are bash examples in documentation blocks. Network and environment variable references are configuration templates for Azure Cosmos DB connectivity. The skill demonstrates proper security practices including RBAC via DefaultAzureCredential, parameterized queries, and secure secret management.
valueStatement: Implement production-grade Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL services with proper authentication, clean architecture, and test-driven development. This skill provides templates and patterns for secure database integration in FastAPI applications.
userTitle: Build Azure Cosmos DB Services with Python
limitations: Only supports Cosmos DB NoSQL API (not SQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, or Table APIs)
Requires Python environment with azure-cosmos and azure-identity packages
Async operations wrap synchronous Cosmos SDK calls via thread pool
Emulator mode requires local Azure Cosmos DB emulator installation

useCases.title: Backend Developer Building SaaS Application
useCases.description: Implement multi-tenant data storage using Cosmos DB with partition key isolation per workspace, leveraging DefaultAzureCredential for secure production deployment.

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Development Environment
useCases.description: Configure local development with Cosmos DB emulator, using environment variables for endpoint configuration and test fixtures for CI/CD pipelines.

useCases.title: Solution Architect Designing Cloud-Native Applications
useCases.description: Apply clean architecture patterns with repository and service layers, ensuring separation of business logic from database implementation for long-term maintainability.

promptTemplates.title: Basic: Set Up Cosmos DB Client
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Cosmos DB client module with singleton pattern that uses DefaultAzureCredential for production and emulator key for local development. Include SSL configuration and container initialization.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new FastAPI project with Azure Cosmos DB

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Create Service Layer
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a service class for Project entity with CRUD operations. Use five-tier Pydantic model pattern and implement graceful degradation when Cosmos DB is unavailable.
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing business logic layer for entity management

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Write Parameterized Queries
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement Cosmos DB SQL queries using @parameter syntax for filtering projects by workspace_id and name. Include proper error handling for CosmosResourceNotFoundError.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building secure queries that prevent injection attacks

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Set Up Testing Infrastructure
promptTemplates.prompt: Create pytest fixtures for mocking Cosmos DB container operations. Include async test examples for service layer methods with proper mocking of get_container function.
promptTemplates.scenario: Writing unit tests without actual Cosmos DB connection

bestPractices: Always use DefaultAzureCredential in production environments - never hardcode keys or secrets in source code
Use parameterized queries with @parameter syntax to prevent SQL injection in Cosmos DB queries
Implement graceful degradation by returning None or empty lists when Cosmos DB is unavailable instead of throwing exceptions
antiPatterns: Hardcoding connection keys or secrets directly in source code instead of using environment variables or managed identity
Using string concatenation to build queries instead of parameterized query syntax with @parameter placeholders
Creating new CosmosClient instances per request instead of using singleton pattern for connection pooling

faq.question: What is the difference between DefaultAzureCredential and emulator key authentication?
faq.answer: DefaultAzureCredential uses managed identity or Azure CLI credentials for production environments with RBAC. Emulator key authentication uses a well-known key for local development only and should never be used in production.

faq.question: How do I handle Cosmos DB unavailability gracefully?
faq.answer: Implement a _use_cosmos() check in your service layer that returns False when the container is None. Service methods return None for single items or empty lists for queries instead of raising exceptions.

faq.question: Why use async wrappers for synchronous Cosmos SDK calls?
faq.answer: The Azure Cosmos DB Python SDK is synchronous. Using starlette's run_in_threadpool prevents blocking the FastAPI event loop, maintaining async performance characteristics.

faq.question: What is the five-tier model pattern?
faq.answer: It uses ProjectBase for shared fields, ProjectCreate for creation requests, ProjectUpdate for partial updates, Project for API responses, and ProjectInDB for internal documents with docType metadata.

faq.question: How do I choose a partition key for Cosmos DB?
faq.answer: Select a partition key that provides even data distribution and aligns with query patterns. For multi-tenant apps, workspace_id is common. Validate partition key access matches user authorization to enforce data boundaries.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with other Azure Cosmos DB APIs like MongoDB or SQL?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on the NoSQL API with native Cosmos SDK. MongoDB and SQL APIs require different client libraries and query patterns, though the service layer architecture principles remain applicable.
